skill.name: literature-review
skill.description: Conduct systematic literature reviews across academic databases, organize search strategies, verify citations, and produce structured review documents with optional PDF output.
skill.summary: Create structured, citation-checked literature reviews from multi-database research searches.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most Markdown backtick, DOI, and weak-crypto hits are false positives from documentation examples. Confirmed risks are legitimate but material: the skill allows Bash, runs Pandoc/XeLaTeX through subprocess, makes outbound DOI/Crossref requests, and writes local output files. No evidence found of credential theft, hidden exfiltration, obfuscation, or prompt injection attempts.

medium_findings.title: External PDF Tool Execution
medium_findings.description: The PDF helper invokes Pandoc and dependency checks through subprocess. The calls use argument lists rather than shell=True, which reduces command injection risk, but publishing should warn users that local external binaries execute on user-supplied Markdown paths and citation style inputs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct subprocess.run calls are present and semantically tied to PDF generation. Risk is moderate because arguments are passed as lists and no shell execution was found.

medium_findings.title: Outbound Citation Verification Requests
medium_findings.description: The citation verifier creates a requests session and sends DOI values to doi.org and Crossref. This is expected for citation checking, but users should know that identifiers from their documents leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network behavior is explicit and the destination domains match the stated citation-verification purpose. I found no evidence that secrets or unrelated files are transmitted.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Local File Output
medium_findings.description: The search and citation scripts write result files to paths derived from command-line input. This is normal for report generation, but it can overwrite local files if users provide sensitive paths.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are clear and purposeful, but there is no path traversal exploit by itself because paths come from local command-line use. The concern is operational file overwrite risk.

low_findings.title: Documentation Patterns Misclassified as Execution
low_findings.description: Many static findings marked Markdown fences, inline code, Boolean search examples, and review templates as Ruby backtick execution, weak cryptography, or reconnaissance. These are documentation examples, not executable code paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown prose or fenced examples, and they are not interpreted by the skill as code. The static labels do not match the semantic context.

low_findings.title: Benign Reference URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Several hardcoded URLs point to academic style guides, DOI examples, PRISMA, PubMed, and other research resources. They support the skill purpose and do not show hidden network activity by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible user-facing references and citation examples. I did not find evidence of disguised callbacks or suspicious destinations.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run Invokes Local Binaries
dangerous_patterns.description: Pandoc and XeLaTeX dependency checks and PDF generation execute local binaries. This is useful for the skill but should be treated as a privileged local action.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous pattern is directly present. Context shows a legitimate PDF conversion purpose rather than malicious command execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: HTTP Requests to External Metadata Services
dangerous_patterns.description: The verifier sends DOI lookups to public metadata services. This can disclose document DOI lists to those services.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The requests are explicit and destination domains are visible. The data sent is limited to DOI strings based on observed code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts save formatted search results and detailed citation reports to disk. Users should review output paths before running them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The writes are direct and easy to verify. They are expected behavior, but still relevant for marketplace risk disclosure.

content.value_statement: Literature reviews require repeatable searches, clear synthesis, and accurate citations. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through database search planning, citation verification, and structured review drafting.
actual_capabilities: Plans systematic search strategies across PubMed, arXiv, bioRxiv, Semantic Scholar, and related databases.
Provides a structured literature review template with methods, results, discussion, references, and appendices.
Processes exported search results into Markdown, BibTeX, or JSON summaries.
Deduplicates papers by DOI or title and ranks results by citations, year, or relevance score.
Verifies DOI metadata through doi.org and Crossref and writes a citation report.
Generates PDFs from Markdown when Pandoc and XeLaTeX are installed locally.
limitations: It does not replace human screening, quality assessment, or domain expert judgment.
Database searches may require separate tools, API access, or manual export steps.
Citation verification sends DOI values to external metadata services.
PDF generation depends on local Pandoc and LaTeX installations.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Systematic Review Protocol
use_cases.description: Define the research question, inclusion criteria, databases, search strings, and documentation plan before searching.

use_cases.title: Synthesize Evidence for a Manuscript
use_cases.description: Turn exported article lists into a thematic review with citation checks and documented search decisions.

use_cases.title: Audit References Before Submission
use_cases.description: Verify DOI metadata, format citations, and generate a final review document for review or publication.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Review Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the literature-review skill to plan a review on [topic]. Define the research question, databases, inclusion criteria, exclusion criteria, and expected outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Build Search Strings
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the literature-review skill to create database-specific search strings for [research question]. Include PubMed, arXiv or bioRxiv, Semantic Scholar, and one specialized database.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize Exported Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the literature-review skill to synthesize the papers in [results file]. Deduplicate results, rank important papers, group findings by theme, and identify evidence gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Finalize a Review Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the literature-review skill to verify citations in [review file], prepare a PRISMA-style methods summary, and generate a polished Markdown review with PDF-ready structure.

output_examples.input: A request to review CRISPR delivery methods for sickle cell disease from 2019 to 2026.
output_examples.output: A review plan with PICO framing, PubMed and bioRxiv search strings, inclusion criteria, exclusion criteria, and a documentation checklist.

output_examples.input: A combined search export containing duplicate records and mixed citation formats.
output: A citation verification summary with failed DOI checks highlighted.

output_examples.input: A draft literature review that needs publication-ready structure.
output_examples.output: A structured Markdown review with abstract, methods, results, discussion, references, appendices, and PDF generation guidance.

best_practices: Document every database, search string, date range, and result count before screening.
Verify citations and DOI metadata before using generated references in formal work.
Use human reviewers for eligibility decisions, quality assessment, and final conclusions.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on a single database for a comprehensive review.
Do not treat preprints as peer-reviewed evidence without clear labeling.
Do not run PDF generation or citation verification on sensitive files without reviewing network and file outputs.

faq.question: Can this skill perform a complete systematic review automatically?
faq.answer: No. It supports planning, formatting, deduplication, citation checks, and synthesis, but humans must validate study selection and conclusions.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does citation verification use the network?
faq.answer: Yes. The verifier sends DOI lookups to doi.org and Crossref to retrieve metadata.

faq.question: Can it create PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes, when Pandoc and XeLaTeX are installed on the local system.

faq.question: What citation styles are covered?
faq.answer: The included references cover APA, Nature, Vancouver, Chicago, and IEEE-style guidance.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning for Bash access, external command execution, network citation checks, and local file writes.
